library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)


raw_df <- read_csv("data/processed/species.csv")


clean_df <- raw_df %>% column_to_rownames(var = "Sample_ID")

metadata <- clean_df %>% select(Participant_ID, Sample_type, Study_group_new, Fiber_restriction)
mycobiome_data <- clean_df %>% select(starts_with("s__"))

# Calculate the distance matrix
# method = "bray" for Bray-Curtis
dist_matrix <- vegdist(mycobiome_data, method = "bray")

# Run the PERMANOVA using the adonis2 function
# The formula is: distance_matrix ~ grouping_variable
permanova_result <- adonis2(dist_matrix ~ Study_group_new, 
                            data = metadata, 
                            permutations = 999)

# View the p-value and R-squared (how much variance is explained by disease group)
print(permanova_result)

# Calculate PCoA coordinates
pcoa_calc <- cmdscale(dist_matrix, k = 2, eig = TRUE)

# Extract the X and Y coordinates for plotting
pcoa_coords <- as.data.frame(pcoa_calc$points)
colnames(pcoa_coords) <- c("PCoA1", "PCoA2")

# Calculate the percentage of variance explained by each axis (for your axis labels)
eigenvalues <- pcoa_calc$eig
variance_explained <- round(eigenvalues / sum(eigenvalues) * 100, 1)

# Bind the coordinates 
plot_data <- cbind(pcoa_coords, metadata)

# Build the plot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, color = Study_group_new)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  stat_ellipse(level = 0.95, linetype = "dashed") + # Adds 95% confidence intervals around groups
  labs(
    title = "Species Beta Diversity",
    subtitle = paste("PERMANOVA p-value =", permanova_result$`Pr(>F)`[1]),
    x = paste0("PCoA 1 (", variance_explained[1], "%)"),
    y = paste0("PCoA 2 (", variance_explained[2], "%)")
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
